inspect-unstaged-changes-with-diff.label: Inspect Unstaged Changes with Diff
inspect-unstaged-changes-with-diff.summary: Move from the file map in status to line-level evidence, then separate unrelated edits before staging.

try_inspect_unstaged_changes_with_diff_s.title: Read One Unstaged Edit
try_inspect_unstaged_changes_with_diff_s.prompt:
You are working in the prepared `community-site/` repository.

The community site heading changed in `index.html`. Inspect only that file’s unstaged diff.
try_inspect_unstaged_changes_with_diff_s.hint: Inspect the working-tree patch before changing the staging area.

help.concept: git diff shows working-tree changes that have not been staged.
help.hint_1: Focus on the removed and added lines for the path named in the task.
help.hint_2: The patch should contain only the edit described in the scenario.

zoeskoul_setup_sh.content:
#!/usr/bin/env bash set -eu rm -rf community-site mkdir -p community-site git -C community-site init -q -b main git -C community-site config --local user.name "ZoeSkoul Learner" git -C community-site config --local user.email "learner@zoeskoul.local" cd community-site printf %s '

Community Hub

' > index.html cd .. git -C community-site add -A git -C community-site commit -q -m 'Add project baseline' cd community-site printf %s '

Neighborhood Hub

' > index.html cd ..

try_inspect_unstaged_changes_with_diff_s.title: List the Files That Changed
try_inspect_unstaged_changes_with_diff_s.prompt:
You are working in the prepared `community-site/` repository.

Two tracked files changed. Use the name-only diff view first, then inspect `app.js` in detail.
try_inspect_unstaged_changes_with_diff_s.hint: Inspect the working-tree patch before changing the staging area.

help.concept: The name-only view reveals the scope of an edit before a detailed diff explains one file.
help.hint_1: Focus on the removed and added lines for the path named in the task.
help.hint_2: The patch should contain only the edit described in the scenario.

zoeskoul_setup_sh.content:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -eu
rm -rf community-site
mkdir -p community-site
git -C community-site init -q -b main
git -C community-site config --local user.name "ZoeSkoul Learner"
git -C community-site config --local user.email "learner@zoeskoul.local"
cd community-site
printf %s 'const open = false;
' > app.js
cd ..
cd community-site
printf %s 'export const theme = "light";
' > config.js
cd ..
git -C community-site add -A
git -C community-site commit -q -m 'Add project baseline'
cd community-site
printf %s 'const open = true;
' > app.js
cd ..
cd community-site
printf %s 'export const theme = "dark";
' > config.js
cd ..

#!/usr/bin/env bash

git -C community-site config --local user.email "learner@zoeskoul.local"
cd community-site
printf %s 'const open = false;
' > app.js
cd ..
cd community-site
printf %s 'export const theme = "light";
' > config.js
cd ..
git -C community-site add -A
git -C community-site commit -q -m 'Add project baseline'
cd community-site
printf %s 'const open = true;
' > app.js
cd ..
cd community-site
printf %s 'export const theme = "dark";
' > config.js
cd ..

try_inspect_unstaged_changes_with_diff_s.title: Review Before You Stage
try_inspect_unstaged_changes_with_diff_s.prompt:
You are working in the prepared `community-site/` repository.

The volunteer instructions changed in `README.md`. Read the diff and then use short status; leave the change unstaged.
try_inspect_unstaged_changes_with_diff_s.hint: Inspect the working-tree patch before changing the staging area.

help.concept: Reviewing before staging helps keep accidental or unrelated edits out of the next snapshot.
help.hint_1: Focus on the removed and added lines for the path named in the task.
help.hint_2: The patch should contain only the edit described in the scenario.

try_inspect_unstaged_changes_with_diff_s.starterCode: Open Saturdays from 9 AM to noon.

git -C community-site config --local user.email "learner@zoeskoul.local"

printf %s 'Open Saturdays.
' > README.md
cd ..
git -C community-site add -A
git -C community-site commit -q -m 'Add project baseline'
cd community-site
printf %s 'Open Saturdays from 9 AM to noon.
' > README.md
cd ..

single-choice-1.title: Using git diff
single-choice-1.hint: Focus on what the command or concept observes or changes at this point in the workflow.

help.concept: Move from a file-level status report to a line-level review, and separate unrelated edits before they reach the staging area.
help.hint_1: Remove options that act on a different repository layer than the one named in the question.
help.hint_2: Choose the answer that remains accurate even when other unrelated files exist.

multi-choice-2.title: Interpreting Diff Output
multi-choice-2.prompt: Which symbol in `git diff` output indicates a line has been added?
multi-choice-2.hint: Choose the one answer that describes this command or concept precisely.

help.hint_1: Remove choices that act on a different repository layer or promise behavior Git does not provide.
help.hint_2: Prefer the exact definition used in this chapter over a nearby Git idea.

sketch0.title: Open the Line-Level Evidence
sketch0.bodyMarkdown:
Short status names the changed path. A plain diff shows how its working copy differs from the staging area:

```bash
git diff -- menu.txt
```

Removed lines begin with `-`; added lines begin with `+`. Those markers describe the comparison and are not stored in the file. Read the body of the patch first, then use the surrounding context to confirm the location.

sketch2.title: Separate Unrelated Edits
sketch2.bodyMarkdown:
Several valid edits do not automatically belong in one snapshot. List the changed tracked paths with `git diff --name-only`, then open one path at a time.

This is not a new workflow command; it is a more deliberate use of diff. The goal is to discover commit boundaries while the work is still easy to separate.

sketch3.title: Review Before You Stage
sketch3.bodyMarkdown:
Make review the default: use status for the map and diff for the detail. The pair catches debug text, unfinished wording, and unrelated edits before they enter the staging area.

Later exercises will reuse diff at staged and project boundaries without reintroducing what a patch is.
